name: Buf Schema Registry API
what_it_does: Protobuf schema registry and toolchain with REST/gRPC API — managing .proto files with lint enforcement, breaking change detection, remote schema repositories, generated SDK distribution, and Buf Schema Registry (BSR) for sharing and versioning Protobuf APIs.
use_cases: Hosting and versioning .proto files in Buf Schema Registry (BSR) for team consumption
Enforcing Protobuf style guide and breaking change detection in CI pipelines via Buf CLI
Distributing generated gRPC client SDKs (Go, TypeScript, Python) directly from BSR
Managing API compatibility across service versions with automated breaking change detection
Integrating Protobuf schema management into Gradle, Bazel, or cargo build pipelines
not_for: REST/JSON API management without Protobuf/gRPC usage
Teams not using Protocol Buffers as API definition format
Simple projects that don't need schema registry or code generation distribution
best_when: A team using gRPC and Protocol Buffers needs centralized schema management, breaking change detection, and generated SDK distribution across multiple services and consumers.
avoid_when: You use REST/OpenAPI or don't use Protobuf — Buf is Protobuf-specific tooling.

auth.notes: BSR API tokens for registry operations. Repository and organization-scoped access. Read vs write token scopes. CLI authenticates via `buf registry login`. CI tokens recommended over personal tokens.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: public repositories unlimited; 1 private repository
pricing.notes: Open source Buf CLI (Apache 2.0). BSR cloud is freemium. US-based startup with strong VC backing. Rapidly becoming the standard for Protobuf tooling. Strong ecosystem: Buf Connect for gRPC-Web. CNCF member project.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits per plan; public BSR has liberal limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST and gRPC APIs for BSR operations (push, pull, list modules). CLI is primary interface. Generated SDK distribution via BSR is agent-accessible. Breaking change detection results via API. Good documentation with clear error messages.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Module push with same content idempotent (content-addressed). Schema reads always idempotent. Breaking change detection is deterministic for same input.
known_agent_gotchas: CLI is primary interface — API is supplementary; most operations via buf CLI commands
Module versioning uses commit hashes — reference by label (tag) for stability
Private repositories require authentication — public repos accessible without token
Breaking change detection rules configurable — default rules may flag false positives

security_score_components.security_notes: Apache 2.0 CLI — fully auditable. SOC2 certified. GDPR compliant. TLS enforced. Token scopes for read/write separation. Content-addressed storage ensures integrity. No secrets in proto files. US data residency.
